curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: People ask me, 'Rodney, how do you deal with the rejection?' I say, the trick is you gotta be drunk enough that you think it went well. I drove home from a date once singing to myself. Found out later she'd gotten out at a red light.
quotes.speaker: Rodney Dangerfield
quotes.context: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson, NBC, October 1981 — pre-show green room, caught on a warm-up mic
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Rodney's no-respect persona and self-loathing relationship humor is exactly this shape, and the 'red light' punchline is perfectly timed. The tell is the driving detail; Rodney almost never set his jokes in a car, he preferred the home or the bedroom as his theatre of humiliation.

quotes.text: My rider says two bottles of red, room temperature, and a cheese plate. You know what I got last night? A bucket of ice, one bottle of something pink with a screw-top, and what I can only describe as a plate of regret. I ate it. I'm not proud. It was that or the minibar.
quotes.speaker: John Candy
quotes.context: SCTV cast reunion panel, Just for Laughs Festival, Montreal, July 1991
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — John Candy's self-deprecating food-and-comfort humor is exactly this shape, and 'a plate of regret' sounds like something he'd say. The giveaway is the rider-complaint framing — Candy was famously easygoing on set and rarely complained publicly about logistics, which makes the diva-rider setup slightly off for him.

quotes.text: People always say, 'How did you keep the audience for four hours?' I say, because I didn't let them leave. You take the intermission away, you control the bladder, you control the room. That's power. Also I had a very good second act. But mostly the bladder thing.
quotes.speaker: Sam Kinison
quotes.context: Penthouse magazine feature interview conducted at the Comedy Store, Los Angeles, published November 1988
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Kinison's bombastic, confrontational stage-control philosophy and his way of mixing genuine craft insight with absurdist aggression is captured here. The giveaway is the 'bladder thing' — it's a slightly too-polished callback; Kinison's real punchlines were louder and more chaotic, not this neatly structured.

quotes.text: You've baked a really lovely cake, but then you've used dog shit for frosting.
quotes.speaker: Steve Jobs
quotes.context: Critiquing a NeXT programmer's work during a project review

source.title: Book 'The Second Coming of Steve Jobs' (Cult of Mac)

quotes.text: I was doing a little over $1,000 a day in heroin, and then probably the same in cocaine and alcohol.
quotes.speaker: Nikki Sixx
quotes.context: Mötley Crüe bassist describing the scale of his addiction at his worst, around the period of his December 1987 heroin overdose; said in 2019 press for the Netflix film 'The Dirt'.

quotes.text: Why me? Ten million motherfuckers freebasing, and I'm the one who blows up!
quotes.speaker: Richard Pryor
quotes.context: Pryor's frank, self-lacerating bit about the 1980 freebasing accident in which he set himself on fire, from his stand-up film 'Richard Pryor: Live on the Sunset Strip' (1982).
